summary: I was born in a small town called Tarímbaro in Michoacán, Mexico. Although I can't remember much about my early life there since I moved to Minnesota when I was three, Tarímbaro is where my roots are. Later, when I returned to Mexico as a teenager, I experienced life in a small neighborhood similar to where Tarímbaro is, with close-knit community living.

Michoacán is the state in Mexico where I was born, specifically in the town of Tarímbaro. I left Michoacán as a toddler to live in Minnesota but returned there as a teenager to stay with my grandma for about five years. During that time, I adapted to life and school in Michoacán, learned more Spanish, and worked in a family business.

Minnesota has been my home since I was three years old, after my parents moved here from Mexico. I grew up here, went to school, and made friends in Minnesota, especially in St. Paul. After spending several years back in Mexico, I returned to Minnesota in 2016 and now live in Mounds View, continuing my high school education and planning my future here.

I lived in St. Paul, Minnesota during my early childhood and school years. It was where I first learned English with the help of friends like Luis and Raina. I enjoyed going to school there and spending time with my friends before moving back to Mexico as a teenager.

Mexico is where I was born and where I spent part of my childhood and teenage years. After moving to Minnesota as a toddler, I returned to Mexico at thirteen to stay with my grandma for about five years. Living there was very different from the U.S., with brick houses, busy cities, and transportation like Metrobús. I worked in my uncle's family business and enjoyed cultural events like the Carnival festival every February.

While living in Mexico, I noticed that many people got around using the Metrobús, taxis, or by walking. It was a common way to navigate the busy city streets, which were very different from Minnesota. The Metrobús was part of daily life and transportation during my years there.

My favorite holiday in Mexico was the Carnival, which took place every February. It was a big festival where people from nearby towns came together, and even TV channels would record the event. The day was crowded with fundraisers for a Carnival queen and lots of drinks, making it a lively and memorable celebration for me.

After returning to Minnesota in October 2016, I started living in Mounds View. It was different from my childhood home in St. Paul, and at first, I had no friends and found it challenging to finish my senior year. Despite the difficulties, I am grateful to be back and am enjoying my last weeks of high school with new friends and activities.
